project.title: Identification of 60S pre-rRNA binding sites of human GNL3L GTPase
project.projectDescription: We reveal that the GNL3 paralogue GTPase, GNL3L, associates with pre-ribosomes. We map RNA contact sites on GNL3L as well as GNL3L binding sites on pre-rRNAs. Lack of GNL3L impairs synthesis of the 60S rRNAs and expression of GTPase inactive GNL3L causes defects in early steps of pre-rRNA processing. Impaired GTP hydrolysis by GNL3L leads to its accumulation on pre-60S particles, together with other assembly factors with proximal binding sites.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: HEK293 Flp-InTM T-RExTM cells expressing GNL3L-His6-2xFlag were irradiated with 2400 mJ/cm2 UV light at 254 nm. Cells were resuspended in IP buffer (50 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.4, 150 mM NaCl, 0.5 mM EDTA, 0.1% Triton-X-100, 10% glycerol, 1x EDTA-free cOmpleteTM Mini Protease Inhibitor Cocktail (Roche)) and lysed by sonication. The lysate was cleared by centrifugation and the supernatant was incubated with anti-FLAG® M2 magnetic beads (Sigma-Aldrich) pre-equilibrated with IP buffer for 3 h at 4°C. After washing with IP buffer, protein–RNA complexes were eluted with 250 µg/mL 3x FLAG® peptide (Sigma-Aldrich) in buffer with 25 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.4, 287 mM NaCl and 2.7 mM KCl. Protein-RNA complexes complexes were ethanol precipitated, pellet were resuspended in 4 M urea and 50 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.5, then diluted four-fold with 50 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.5. Samples were incubated with 500 U PierceTM Universal Nuclease (Thermo Fisher Scientific) and 200 U Nuclease P1 (New England Biolabs) at 37 °C for 2 h. Then, 10 μg RNase A (EN0531, Thermo Fisher Scientific) and 1 U RNase T1 (Thermo Fisher Scientific) were added to sample and further incubated for 2 h at 37°C. Proteins were digested using 4 μg Trypsin (Promega) at 37°C overnight. Samples were purified twice using C18 micro SpinColumnsTM (Harvard Apparatus) according to the manufacturer’s instructions and eluates were dried in a speed vac concentrator. Crosslinked peptides were enriched using TiO2 columns (in-house; Titansphere 5 μm; GL Sciences), as previously described. Samples dissolved in buffer C (5% (v/v) glycerol, 80% (v/v) ACN, 5% (v/v) trifluoroacetic acid (TFA)) were loaded onto equilibrated TiO2 columns, and columns were washed three times with buffer A, three times with buffer D (60% (v/v) ACN, 5% (v/v) TFA) and once with 60% (v/v) ACN, 0.1% (v/v) TFA. Ammonia solution was used to elute samples and eluates were dried in a speed vac concentrator before LC-ESI-MS/MS analysis. After enrichment, peptides crosslinked to (oligo)nucleotide were dissolved in 2% (v/v) ACN, 0.05% (v/v) TFA. LC–MS / MS analyses were performed on an Orbitrap Exploris 480 (Thermo Fisher Scientific) instrument coupled to a nanoflow liquid chromatography system (UltiMate™3000 RSLCnano, Thermo Fisher Scientific). Sample was separated over 58 min at a flow rate of 300 nl/min using 0.1% (v/v) FA (buffer C) and 80% (v/v) ACN, 0.08% (v/v) FA (buffer D) and a linear gradient from 10% to 45% buffer D in 44 min. Eluting (oligo)nucleotide crosslinked peptides were analyzed in positive mode using a data-dependent top 20 acquisition method. Resolution was set to 120,000 (MS1) and 30,000 FWHM (MS2); scan range (m/z), 350–1600; AGC targets, 10 6 (MS1) and 10 5 (MS2); normalized collision energy, 28%; dynamic exclusion, 9 s; maximum injection time, 60 (MS1) and 120 ms (MS2). Measurements were performed in two replicate injections.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: RNA protein crosslinks were identified using OpenMS NuXL v.3.0.0 (UV-UCGA-Extended workflow) with search against the reference Uniprot human proteome database (released on 2023-05-19). MS data were analyzed and manually validated using the OpenMS pipeline NuXL and OpenMS TOPPASViewer. Crosslink sites are reported at 1% spectrum level FDR. Crosslink sites are reported at 1% spectrum level FDR. Mass spectra of crosslinks were manually curated using OpenMS TOPPASView.
